name: vMix Caller
description:
Full Zoom caller management for vMix, with integrated Zoom API control. Supports multi-instance vMix setups and works through NAT — no direct hub-to-site connectivity required.

Architecture: hub/client. Commands are queued on the hub; the client node alongside vMix polls for them and executes against the local vMix API, then reports participants and connection status back. The client page is the primary operator surface — it includes the full admin UI (meeting controls, live participants from vMix, saved meetings, Zoom API meetings) matching the hub page.

vMix controls: join/leave meeting, mute/unmute self, toggle camera, mute all participants, Put On Air (ZoomSelectParticipantByName). Live participants list polled directly from vMix and reported to the hub. vMix IP, port, and input index are configured on the hub and pushed to the client node automatically on save. Supports multiple named vMix instances — switch active instance from both hub and client pages.

Zoom API (Phase 1 — hub as bridge): Register a Server-to-Server OAuth app at marketplace.zoom.us (meeting:read:admin + meeting:write:admin scopes) and enter Account ID, Client ID, and Client Secret in the hub credentials panel. The hub holds and refreshes the Bearer token (1-hour cache); client nodes proxy all Zoom requests through the hub using HMAC-signed GET and approval-only POST — Zoom credentials never leave the hub. Features: live meetings list (upcoming + in-progress, 60 s cache), one-click Join (passes meeting ID to vMix), End meeting for all, +Save to presenter presets, Create meeting inline form (topic, passcode, duration, waiting room option).

Video preview (optional): SRT-to-HLS bridge — LAN mode (vMix pushes SRT to local machine; browser accesses directly) or hub-server mode (client fetches TS segments and pushes to hub; hub serves a synthetic HLS manifest through a session-authenticated proxy so remote HTTPS browsers can view without reaching the LAN).

Presenter view (/hub/vmixcaller/presenter): clean bookmark-friendly page for the studio email machine. One-click join from saved meetings list, full-screen video feed, Hear Caller audio, mute/camera/leave controls, Reconnect button. No technical config needed.

Saved meetings: store named meeting presets (ID + passcode) for quick one-click join from the presenter view or operator page. Import directly from the Zoom API meetings list with +Save.

Note: Teams has no vMix API for call control; Teams NDI feeds already in vMix can be monitored passively but not joined or controlled by this plugin. Keyboard shortcuts: M = mute self, C = toggle camera.

name: Brand Screen
description: Animated full-screen studio branding display. Studios (physical screens) are assigned Stations (brand configs) — change the assignment instantly via REST API and all browsers update in real time over SSE. Per-station logo upload, brand colour, background style (particles, aurora, waves, beams, grid, burst, haze, minimal, live video), and logo animation (orbit rings, pulse, glow, float, spin, glitch, bounce). Background hue derived from brand colour — the entire screen lives in your brand palette. Logo fills most of the screen. Assign a live audio stream to animate orbit rings, spin speed, bounce kicks, pulse tempo, bloom, beams, burst rays, grid lines, and particles with real audio levels at 150 ms. Broadcast clock, On Air badge, live now-playing from Zetta or any JSON API, message-from-hub amber banner. Token-auth kiosk URL per studio. Yodeck compatible. Anti-burn-in pixel drift. Schedule different brands to take over studios at specific days/times or one-off on a specific date — auto-reverts when the window ends. Producer login gets write access to Brand Screen. CueServer LED integration — per-brand brightness slider (0–100), per-studio zone name for ZONE/Preset command format, multi-strip DMX config. Scene discovery auto-saves the command — no manual typing required. Video brand screen — select a vMix Caller SRS bridge source; the brand screen connects directly via WebRTC/WHEP on the same LAN, no hub relay, sub-second latency. Hub-only.

name: 25/7 PDM
description: 25/7 Programme Delay Manager monitor. Installed on client nodes — connects to the PDM unit via TCP, polls delay depth at 1 Hz, and pushes live data to the hub every 2 s. Hub displays a pill per reporting site showing delay in ms and frame equivalents at 25/30 fps. Configurable IP address and port.

name: FM Scanner
description: Live FM radio receiver with RDS display, frequency history, presets, and band scan. Streams audio from a remote site's RTL-SDR dongle (role=scanner) to the browser in real time.

name: Web SDR
description: Browser-based software defined radio with scrolling waterfall display. Tune any frequency within the capture bandwidth. WFM, NFM, and AM demodulation modes. Requires an RTL-SDR dongle on the client machine.

name: DAB Scanner
description: DAB digital radio browser. Scans all Band III channels to discover services and streams selected services as MP3 audio. Displays DLS scrolling text. Raspberry Pi supported. Requires welle-cli and ffmpeg on the client machine.

name: Logger
description: Compliance logger for continuous 24/7 recording of all monitored streams. Hub mode aggregates recordings from all connected sites — browse timelines, play audio, and export segments with mark in/out. Supports now-playing metadata from Planet Radio, Triton, Zetta, or any JSON API. Requires ffmpeg.

name: Meter Wall
description: Full-screen audio meter wall showing live PPM-style level bars for every monitored stream across all connected sites. Peak hold, LUFS readout, alert flash, RDS/DLS now-playing, site grouping, and kiosk mode. Stereo L/R bars at 150 ms update rate.

name: Livewire
description: Axia Livewire source discovery. Listens for LWAP advertisements on 239.192.255.3:4001 and builds a live inventory of sources across all connected sites — Node, Stream ID, friendly name, multicast address, and online/stale status. Client nodes push their source tables to the hub every 30 s. Sources grouped by node with accordion display, sortable columns (Stream ID, Last Seen, Status), and live search. Stale-source alerts via email, webhook, and push with per-channel configuration. Create Input buttons on client nodes. Hub-only overview shows all sites.

name: Zetta Integration
description: RCS Zetta broadcast automation integration. Multi-instance SOAP monitor — shows live sequencer state per station: mode (Auto/Manual/Voice Track), status (PLAYING/QUEUED/NEXT), GAP, ETM, now-playing title/artist/cart/category, and upcoming queue. Detects commercial/spot blocks in real time. Per-station broadcast chain assignment with live status chips on the Broadcast Chains page. Exposes chain now-playing data to Logger on client recording nodes. Includes a SOAP debug explorer. Requires zeep.

name: Morning Report
description: Daily morning briefing auto-generated at 06:00 covering the previous day. Plain-English fault summary, per-chain health table with on-air %, traffic-light trend pills, hourly fault heatmap, stream quality summary, Zetta automation health section, and a full per-outage detail log showing fault time, duration, Zetta computer, automation mode, what was playing, and which studio/brand was live at the time. Hub-only.

name: Signal Path Latency
description: Signal path latency tracking for broadcast chains. Polls all connected sites for comparator delay measurements, stores 90 days of history, and displays per-comparator sparklines with stable/drifting/alert badges. Hub-only.

name: Codec Monitor
description: Real-time connection monitor for broadcast contribution codecs — Comrex ACCESS/BRIC-Link, Tieline Gateway/ViA, Prodys Quantum ST, and APT/WorldCast Quantum. Polls via HTTP scraping or SNMP and fires CODEC_FAULT/RECOVERY alerts. Runs on hub and client nodes.

name: Push Server
description: Centralised push notification server for iOS and Android. Holds APNs and FCM credentials and delivers notifications on behalf of any SignalScope installation that points its Push Server URL here. Hub-only.

name: Wallboard
description: TV-optimised broadcast wallboard with live chain status cards, station logos, PPM-style level meters, alert ticker, and full customisation. Upload logos per chain, select which streams to display, toggle LUFS/now-playing/hero banner. Gradient avatars, health bars, zone-coloured meters at 150 ms update rate. Zetta mode badges on chain cards, drag-to-reorder chain cards via settings drawer, anti-burn-in pixel drift. AzuraCast now-playing shown on chain cards when no Zetta is linked. Hub-only.

name: PTP Clock
description: Full-screen GPS-accurate wall clock for broadcast studios. Digital and analog modes, live PTP sync status, offset and jitter display, custom branding via URL parameter.

name: Icecast Streaming
description: Manage Icecast2 streaming servers on client nodes. Re-streams any monitored input to Icecast2. Hub overview shows live listener counts and stream status from all connected sites. Requires ffmpeg; Icecast2 installed separately.

name: Listener
description: Live stream listener for presenters and producers. Station cards with live level meters and one-tap audio playback. Designed for non-technical users — auto-reconnects, animated equalizer, volume control, mobile-friendly. Hub-only.

name: Producer View
description: Simplified hub view for producers and presenters. Station status cards with live levels and plain-English chain fault history. Users assigned the Producer role go directly here on login. Hub-only.

name: AzuraCast
description: AzuraCast web radio integration. Live now-playing data, listener counts, and station health across all connected sites. Fires AZURACAST_FAULT/RECOVERY alerts and optionally cross-references with SignalScope silence detection.

name: IP Link
description: Browser-based IP codec and SIP softphone. WebRTC rooms with shareable talent URLs — low-latency Opus audio, bidirectional IFB, live level meters. Permanent rooms support server-side routing with Livewire/AES67 multicast output. Server-side SIP registration (hub holds multiple SIP accounts permanently — no browser needed). Requires aiortc, av, and numpy for server-side WebRTC.

name: Sync Capture
description: Multi-site synchronized audio capture. Select inputs from any connected sites, set a duration, and capture — the hub timestamps the command and each client grabs its rolling buffer at the same moment. All clips presented together with inline players, EBU R128 analysis, waveforms, and DAW session export (REAPER/Audition). Hub-only.

name: Studio Board
description: Large-format studio display for outside broadcast studios. Shows presenter/show info, now playing, live level meters, mic live status, chain health, and Zetta automation queue. Fully configurable admin page with Studios (physical screens) and Brands (station presets) — assign a brand to a studio in one REST call. REST API for mic live, brand assignment, and legacy chain assignment. Themeable (dark/Bauer/corp via ?theme=), clock header, big presenter countdown timer, message-from-hub banner, anti-burn-in pixel drift. Server-side presenter image cache. Airwave Studio Bookings integration — map local studios to Airwave studios; next confirmed booking (or current session) appears automatically on the TV display when the studio is unassigned. AzuraCast now-playing shown on studio display (artist, title, playlist, progress bar, next track, live badge) when no Zetta is linked. Hub-only.

name: Audio Router
description: Broadcast audio routing — route any monitored input on any connected SignalScope site to a Livewire multicast output or unicast RTP stream on any connected site. Named routes configured in a hub UI; clients execute them autonomously via short-poll. Same-site routes run locally. Cross-site routes use P2P direct streaming when both nodes can reach each other; hub relay is used only as a last resort. DAB and FM inputs are supported via the monitoring PCM buffer. Requires ffmpeg on client nodes. Hub-only overview page.
